Ad Text:
As our Sales Operations Manager you'll play a critical part in improving the efficiency and effectiveness of our sales organisation through robust analytics, insightful reporting and strong sales operations discipline.

Reporting to the Head of Sales Force Effectiveness, this is a fantastic opportunity for someone who enjoys turning data into actionable insights, partnering closely with Sales leaders, and driving continuous improvement across sales performance, reporting and incentives.

About the role

In this role, you will be responsible for strengthening sales operations and analytics capability across the business. You will ensure the sales team has timely, accurate and meaningful insights to support fact-based decision making and profitable growth.

Key responsibilities include:

Driving improvements in sales efficiency and effectiveness through best practice sales methodology and operational rigour

Delivering insightful analysis of merchant, customer and segment performance, identifying opportunities for improvement

Developing, owning and enhancing sales reports, dashboards and performance analytics across all segments

Reviewing and streamlining existing reporting to ensure relevance, efficiency and impact

Establishing and managing a consistent sales reporting cadence, including target and quota reviews

Playing a key role in the management and ongoing improvement of the ANZ Sales Incentive Program

Providing clear performance insights aligned to the sales incentive plan and supporting course-correction where required

Supporting pricing analytics to enable effective pricing mechanisms and profitable volume growth

Leading and developing direct reports, fostering a high-performance and insight-led culture

About you

You are an analytical and commercially savvy sales operations professional who thrives on creating clarity from complexity. You're comfortable working with senior stakeholders and are confident influencing outcomes through data-led insights.

You will bring:

Strong experience in sales operations, analytics or sales performance roles

A proven ability to translate data into practical, actionable insights for sales teams and leaders

Experience building and managing sales dashboards, reports and performance frameworks

A solid understanding of sales incentives, targets and performance management

Strong stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to work cross-functionally

People leadership experience, with a focus on capability development and performance

Why join us?

You will be joining a collaborative and high-performing sales environment where insights genuinely shape decision making.

This role offers visibility, impact and the opportunity to influence how sales performance is measured and optimised across the business.

If you are passionate about sales analytics, enjoy partnering with Sales leaders, and want to make a tangible impact, we would love to hear from you.

This is an excellent opportunity for an energetic, dynamic Senior Commercial Analyst to join a successful, global construction group.

Reporting into the CFO you'll act as a business partner to operational stakeholders, providing commercial, financial & analytical support, to help drive decisions & business performance.

Your analytical scope will encompass areas such as category sales, discounting, gross margins, pre & post promotional analysis and wages. You'll create financial models in order to provide meaningful insights into business performance issues, challenging decisions where necessary.

You'll also prepare and distribute weekly & monthly reports, with associated commentary, for use by the business. And will hold regular meetings with Category Managers and the Commercial Director, to review monthly sales, margin results etc.

Additionally, you'll play a key role in business planning for new stores, budgeting, forecasting and other ad hoc analysis when required.

You are an experienced Financial or Commercial Analyst - who will be ready to step into a Commercial Manager role in the next 1-2 years. Circa 5 years experience in Commercial Finance role/s would be ideal.

You'll have proven analytical skills, combined with a background in business partnering. You will also have advanced Excel / modelling skills, whilst experience using a BI tool and working with large data sets.

For more information, please contact Schenuk Wijesekera on 02 9775 5916. Or to apply, hit the "apply" button.

Job Title: Data Analyst - Multiple Positions - Tier 1 Bank
Ad Text:
We are looking for multiple Data Analyst's to join a high profile program of work in a leading Domestic bank, where you will have a broad audience across the business, technology, risk and finance.

As part of your role you'll work closely with stakeholders to understand and deliver the impact assessment of key projects inflight, how the business needs to adapt, and how to mitigate any undue risk to customers, all from the lens of data and data impacts.

You'll be responsible for:

Gap analysis and recommend new data flows that need to be implemented

Business case analysis through extensive data extraction and interrogation

Develop ETL packages and intuitive PowerBI dashboards for the business

Integrate yourself into agile delivery squads and ensure data requirements are captured

Review data designs and guide technology on data lifecycle management 

You'll play a key role in this transformation team as lead data resource when it comes to assesses the impact of programs of work on the business.

What sort of background do you need to be successful in this role?

Strong competency with SQL and data warehousing experience

Experience with reporting, data visualisation and storytelling insights with tools such as Tableau and Power BI

Experience in Banking/ Fin Services is mandatory

Principal Consumer Insurance (CI) Strategy

Permanent opportunity
Location Sydney (flexible working supported)

Join Suncorp’s Consumer Insurance team in a senior strategy leadership role with meaningful impact across one of Australia’s leading financial services organisations.

About the role

As Principal, Consumer Insurance (CI) Strategy, you will support the development of the Consumer Insurance strategy, ensuring alignment with Group strategy and enterprise priorities. Reporting to the Executive Manager, Consumer Insurance Strategy, this role plays a critical advisory and leadership position, partnering closely with senior leaders to solve complex strategic problems, shape long-term direction, and deliver high-quality insights to Executive Leadership and Boards.

This is a First Line Leader role at Fixed Salary Band 6, offering exposure to senior executives and the opportunity to influence outcomes at scale.

More specifically, you will:

Strategy development

Support the development of the Consumer Insurance strategy in close collaboration with senior leaders, ensuring it is clear, actionable and aligned with Group strategy
Define problem statements and draft proposals to scope activities, milestones, deliverables and engagement model
Lead hypothesis-driven, first-principles problem solving across a wide range of strategic issues
Facilitate strategy workshops, including off-sites, deep dives and ad hoc sessions
Deliver strategic research and insights, including market scans, competitor analysis, deep dives, opportunity assessments and commercial analyses
Build strong cross-functional relationships to ensure integration and alignment of strategic initiatives
Provide coaching and mentoring on junior members of the team (as required)

Business and strategic planning

Support Consumer Business Planning activities in line with Group Strategy milestones
Facilitate alignment between Consumer Insurance plans and broader enterprise objectives
Track progress against strategic milestones, identifying dependencies, risks and trade-offs
Provide clear, data-driven analysis and recommendations to support executive decision-making

Board and executive deliverables

Prepare and deliver high-quality presentations and reports for the Group and Consumer Boards, Executive Leadership Team (ELT), regulators and partners
Ensure timely, accurate and insightful content for key Board meetings, particularly May and November cycles
Clearly articulate strategic priorities, progress, risks and opportunities for Consumer Insurance

Ad hoc strategic analysis

Support additional strategic, commercial or market analysis as required

About you

You are a highly capable strategy professional with strong executive presence, commercial judgement and a passion for solving complex business problems.

Approximately 7+ years’ experience in corporate or business unit strategy, including some leadership experience
At least 5 years’ strategy consulting experience at a top-tier firm strategy house (preferred)
Insurance experience desirable but not essential
Demonstrated experience applying strategic frameworks in corporate or consulting environments
Strong hypothesis-driven and structured problem-solving capability
High level of commercial and financial acumen, including valuation concepts
Advanced analytical, quantitative and qualitative skills
Proven ability to influence and manage senior executive stakeholders
Ability to lead, inspire and collaborate with diverse professional teams
Expert-level written communication, including Board and executive-level materials
Confidence facilitating complex strategic conversations and workshops
Strong data-led decision-making capability

Why join us?

Suncorp supports flexibility in how, when and where work is done, where business needs permit. You’ll be joining a collaborative, purpose-led organisation where strategic thinking is valued and your work directly shapes outcomes for customers and the business.

What we can offer you:

Discounts and offers on a range of insurance products
A range of flexible working and leave options, including 20 weeks paid parental leave, 4 weeks secondary carer parental leave, and unlimited paid emergency response leave.
Invest in your brighter future with ongoing study support and career development programs.
Give back to our communities with payroll giving, donation matching and paid volunteer leave.
Prioritise your work/life balance with our robust employee assistance program and dedicated employee council.

Employee benefits | Suncorp Group

At Suncorp we believe we are our best when our workforce is as diverse, talented and passionate as the communities in which we live and operate, and where our people feel included, valued and connected. We are passionate about inspiring our people by creating a diverse, accessible, and inclusive culture, offering flexible work, career development and internal mobility, and building connected relationships amongst our team members and with our customers.

Key Responsibilities
Design, configure, and deliver SAP Finance solutions across FI/CO, including General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Fixed Assets.
Support SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Bluefield migrations, including selective data transition and hands‑on involvement in data transformation activities.
Analyse current ECC finance processes and structures and translate them into optimised S/4HANA Finance solutions.
Work closely with data migration teams to support financial data validation, reconciliation, and migration.
Collaborate with cross‑functional SAP teams across MM, SD, EAM, and PS to ensure end‑to‑end integration.
Support system integration testing (SIT), UAT, and financial reconciliation activities.
Drive finance process improvement aligned to S/4HANA best practices and Clean Core principles.
Contribute to cutover planning, go‑live activities, and post‑implementation hypercare support.
Required Skills & Experience
8+ years' experience working as an SAP Finance Functional Consultant.
Strong functional expertise across SAP FI/CO.
Hands‑on experience with GL, AP, AR, Fixed Asset Accounting, and indirect tax frameworks (GST/VAT).
Mandatory experience delivering SAP S/4HANA Bluefield migrations, including selective data transition.
Strong understanding of the S/4HANA Finance data model, including the Universal Journal (ACDOCA).
Proven experience supporting financial data migration, validation, and reconciliation.
Familiarity with SAP Activate methodology and S/4HANA best practices.
Ability to engage effectively with business stakeholders, technical teams, and external delivery partners.
Key Competencies
Strong understanding of end‑to‑end finance business processes.
Ability to translate complex business requirements into clear SAP functional solutions.
Excellent analytical, problem‑solving, and troubleshooting skills.
Experience working on large, complex SAP transformation programs.
Strong communication skills and confidence engaging with senior stakeholders.
Collaborative mindset with the ability to work effectively in cross‑functional SAP teams.
High‑quality documentation and solution design capability.
Proven focus on data integrity and financial accuracy during transformations.
Solid understanding of integrations between finance and logistics modules.
Ability to thrive in fast‑paced project environments and meet delivery milestones.

Why Join?

Join a flagship S/4HANA program, deepen Bluefield expertise, work on complex finance transformations, and accelerate your SAP consulting career.

Get in touch for more information!

Job Title: Solutions Engineer / Pre Sales Consultant (Risk & Audit)New
Ad Text:
Position Overview:

This role is ideal for someone from a Risk, Audit, or Compliance background who enjoys working closely with customers, solving complex problems, and presenting thoughtful, high-impact solutions in a commercial environment.

As a Solutions Engineer at Diligent, you will partner closely with Sales to help organisations understand how our platform can transform the way they manage risk, audit, and compliance. You’ll act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, translating risk and audit challenges into clear, compelling solutions that drive real business value.

If you enjoy combining domain expertise, storytelling, and customer interaction and want to move beyond traditional internal risk or audit roles, this is a strong next step.

Key Responsibilities:

Customer & Commercial Partnership


Partner closely with Sales to support the full pre-sales lifecycle, helping customers understand how Diligent solves real-world risk and audit challenges.

Deliver tailored, high-impact software demonstrations that clearly articulate business value not just product features.

Engage confidently with senior leaders across Audit, Risk, Compliance, IT, and Governance functions.

Translate customer pain points into clear solution narratives that support deal progression and closure.

Solution Design & Thought Leadership


Design and present solution architectures that align with customer objectives and regulatory environments.

Articulate the cross-platform value of the Diligent Platform and how it supports integrated GRC programs.

Collaborate with Product teams to provide customer and domain feedback on product enhancements.

Support marketing initiatives such as webinars, industry events, and thought-leadership content.

Domain Expertise


Apply practical knowledge of Audit, Risk Management, and IT Compliance in customer conversations.

Leverage familiarity with industry frameworks such as COSO ERM, ISO 31000, NIST RMF, and related standards.

Demonstrate how modern GRC platforms enable risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and mitigation at scale.

Required Skills/Experience

Experience in Risk, Audit, Compliance, or GRC, either in a customer-facing role or within industry.

Experience working with SaaS solutions or strong interest in moving into a commercial, customer-facing environment.

Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics.

Comfortable partnering with Sales and contributing to commercial outcomes.

Curious, self-driven, and motivated to continuously deepen both technical and domain knowledge.

#LI-SM1

#LI-Hybrid

About Us

Diligent is the AI leader in governance, risk and compliance (GRC) SaaS solutions, helping more than 1 million users and 700,000 board members to clarify risk and elevate governance. The Diligent One Platform gives practitioners, the C-Suite and the board a consolidated view of their entire GRC practice so they can more effectively manage risk, build greater resilience and make better decisions, faster. 

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast.  Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking.  Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity.  The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together.  At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Learn more at diligent.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook

What Diligent Offers You 

Creativity is ingrained in our culture. We are innovative collaborators by nature. We thrive in exploring how things can be differently both in our internal processes and to help our clients
We care about our people. Diligent offers a flexible work environment, global days of service, comprehensive health benefits, meeting free days, generous time off policy and wellness programs to name a few
We have teams all over the world. We may be headquartered in New York City, but we have office hubs in Washington D.C., Vancouver, London, Galway, Budapest, Munich, Bengaluru, Singapore, and Sydney.
Diversity is important to us. Growing, maintaining and promoting a diverse team is a top priority for us. We foster and encourage diversity through our Employee Resource Groups and provide access to resources and education to support the education of our team, facilitate dialogue, and foster understanding.

Diligent created the modern governance movement. Our world-changing idea is to empower leaders with the technology, insights and connections they need to drive greater impact and accountability – to lead with purpose. Our employees are passionate, smart, and creative people who not only want to help build the software company of the future, but who want to make the world a more sustainable, equitable and better place. 

Headquartered in New York, Diligent has offices in Washington D.C.,  London, Galway, Budapest, Vancouver, Bengaluru, Munich, Singapore and Sydney.   To foster strong collaboration and connection, this role will follow a hybrid work model. If you are within a commuting distance to one of our Diligent office locations, you will be expected to work onsite at least 50% of the time. We believe that in-person engagement helps drive innovation, teamwork, and a strong sense of community.

We are a drug free workplace. Diligent is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, pregnancy, childbirth, physical disability, mental disability, age, military status, protected veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner or civil union status, gender (including sex stereotyping and gender identity or expression), medical condition (including, but not limited to, cancer related or HIV/AIDS related), genetic information, or sexual orientation in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Diligent's EEO Policy and Know Your Rights. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at recruitment@diligent.com.

To all recruitment agencies: Diligent does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Diligent employees or any other organization location. Diligent is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Smartways is scaling quickly, and the complexity of our operation is increasing. Without this role, we add more people to solve problems. With this role, we design the problems out of the system.

At Smartways, Customer Operations is the safety net of our business — ensuring critical healthcare deliveries reach patients, hospitals and clinics exactly when they need to.

We are now looking for someone to take that safety net and make it smarter, faster and more scalable. This is not a reporting role. This is not a traditional workforce planning role.

This is a role for someone who can see how an operation really runs — and redesign it using data, automation and AI.

What you will own

You will sit within our leadership team and take ownership of how our operation is measured, optimised and scaled.

1.   Build Clarity through data

·       Own and evolve our Power BI and reporting environment

·       Bring together multiple systems into a single, trusted view of performance

·       Identify what’s actually driving workload, inefficiency and cost

·       Turn data into decisions — not dashboards

2.   Redesign how the operation works

·       Identify and eliminate unmeasured or unnecessary workload

·       Improve how work is routed, prioritised and completed

·       Build scalable frameworks that reduce reliance on manual effort

·       Model the financial and operational impact of change

3. Lead automation & AI adoption

·       Identify opportunities to automate repetitive or low-value work

·       Design and implement solutions utilising HubSpot, Dialpad and internal systems

·       Introduce AI to improve: Forecasting accuracy, Customer interactions and Task allocation

·       Drive real, measurable ROI from automation initiatives

4. Enable smarter workforce planning

·       Develop forecasts across calls, digital channels and back-office work

·       Design schedules that balance service, cost and team experience

·       Model future resource requirements based on growth and change

·       Improve how we respond to intraday performance challenges

What we’re looking for

We’re not looking for a traditional planner or a passive analyst. We’re looking for someone who:

·       Thinks like an operator, not just an analyst

·       Can move from insight → action → implementation

·       Is comfortable working across systems, data and people

·       Has a strong bias for simplifying and automating

What you will bring

·       Strong analytics capability (Power BI, SQL, Excel or similar)

·       Experience improving or redesigning operational processes

·       Exposure to automation, AI tools or workflow design

·       Experience in contact centres, logistics or service operations (preferred)

·       Workforce planning experience is valuable — but not the core of the role

Why join Smartways

·       Access to the Executive team and real decision-making influence

·       Ownership of high-impact transformation initiatives

·       The opportunity to shape how AI is actually used in operations

·       A role where your work directly improves outcomes for patients and healthcare providers

Category:
General/Business Unit Manager (CEO & General Management)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the role

We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Manager who is driven, focused and passionate about shaping the future of healthcare infrastructure.

As the Manager, Reporting & Analysis you will be responsible for leading and managing an effective team in providing a state-wide analysis and reporting function on financial and non-financial information for capital projects and programs across Queensland Health.

The role provides leadership, guidance, coaching, analysis and advice to Senior Management, the broader Department, and Hospital and Health Services on a broad range of complex, sensitive and time-critical reporting projects. You will have direct reports and will be required to promote an effective team environment that produces quality timely outcomes. 

Key Responsibilities

Provide expert level advice technical leadership on reporting, related projects and issues
Develop and maintain an effective reporting process and system to the delivery of capital projects and programs
Manage the provision of accurate and timely reporting information for government budget cycle requirements

About You

Demonstrated experience leading high-functioning teams within a complex environment.
Strong skills in strategic, conceptional and critical thinking
The ability to analyse a range of financial controls
Experience with data collection and reporting systems, business intelligence tools and programming languages (such as SAP S/4 HANA, Power BI/DAX and SQL).

How to Apply

To apply please submit the following information:

Your current resume, including two referees who have a thorough knowledge of your capabilities, work performance and conduct within the previous two years. It is preferable to include your current, immediate, or past supervisor.
A cover letter (maximum 1-2 pages, dot points acceptable) including the below:
An outline of how your experience, abilities, knowledge and personal qualities would enable you to achieve the key responsibilities and meet the skill requirements of the role.
Responses to the following questions:
Question 1 – What is your motivation to join Health Infrastructure Queensland?
Question 2 – Outline which HIQ commitment you align the most closely with and provide an example of when you have lived this commitment.

Benefits for Joining HIQ:

Attractive and competitive salary with annual incremental increases
Flexible working arrangements 
Corporate discounts and employee wellness programs.
Countless opportunities to learn and ongoing professional development.
Committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace for all employees.
You will contribute to making a difference in the lives of Queenslanders.

We are committed to developing and supporting a workforce that is well equipped and highly motivated to provide responsive and quality services to all Queenslanders. We continue to build an inclusive workplace that embraces diversity of backgrounds and differences to realise the potential of our employees for innovation and delivering services aimed at enhancing the lives of all Queenslanders. 

We encourage applications from Aboriginal people, people with disabilities, LGBTIQ+ and people from culturally diverse backgrounds. Please view our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion here.
